lessons.title: Express Terms and Representations
lessons.description: Here is the core principle. Not every statement made during negotiations becomes a term of the contract.

lessons.title: Conditions, Warranties and Innominate Terms
lessons.description: A condition is a core term — one that goes to the root of the contract.

lessons.title: Implied Terms and Interpretation
lessons.description: Let us start with the core principle on implied terms. Courts may imply a term into a contract where the parties' express agreement is incomplete.

lessons.title: Exemption Clauses
lessons.description: Here is the core principle. An exemption clause is a contractual term by which one party seeks to cut down their liability or limit the other party's remedies.

lessons.title: Certainty of Terms
lessons.description: Here is the core principle. For a contract to be enforceable, its terms must be sufficiently certain.
